one_line: trailing-stop peak ratchets on CLOSE only: updatePosition(pos, row.close) sets highestPrice from the close, never the intra-bar high, so the trail anchor (highestPrice*(1-trailingStopPct), exitRules.ts:244) understates the true peak -> looser/late trailing exits. NOTE: arguably a DELIBERATE conservative choice (close-based trailing avoids intra-bar whipsaw), and there is NO same-bar look-ahead (checkExitConditions receives the pre-update pos, so today's close cannot move today's trail). portfolio alpha compares MISMATCHED windows: alpha = truePortfolioReturn - bnhAvg, where truePortfolioReturn is the END-ALIGNED COMMON (min-length) window combine but bnhAvg = mean of each instrument's FULL-history bnhReturn (core.ts:422 computeBuyAndHoldReturn over all rows). For instruments of unequal length the portfolio leg spans fewer bars than the B&H leg -> alpha is apples-to-oranges. LIVE path (app/api/backtest/route.ts -> aggregatePortfolio) and alpha is shown in the backtest UI. LATENT on the current 56-file dataset (all 1255 bars -> common==full -> alpha currently correct), but /api/backtest accepts arbitrary tickers so mixed-length sets are reachable. Fix = compute bnhAvg over the same end-aligned common window (or compute portfolio return over each instrument's own window); NOT covered by the CI benchmark (which uses benchmarkLabel, not aggregatePortfolio) so a fix cannot be WR-gated
status: FIXED 2026-07-06 (PR #84) — BacktestResult.bnhCurve index-aligned with equityCurve; aggregatePortfolio measures B&H over the SAME end-aligned common window; displayed alpha -104.58%->-89.24% (bnhAvg 104.88->89.55) on the real universe

SSOT consistency: bollingerArray rejects period<2 (returns all-NaN, F2.9 — at period=1 sample variance is identically 0 and the bands collapse to the price line) but bollingerLatest only checks closes.length<period, so period=1 returns degenerate collapsed bands instead of null. Not a live bug (every caller uses the default period=20; unreachable), but the two SSOT entry points should treat period<2 identically. Fix = add `|| period < 2` to the bollingerLatest guard. relativeStrengthVsBenchmark uses INDEX-based lookback (closes[len-22]/[len-64]/[len-127] vs spy[len-22]/[len-64]/[len-127]) and only documents 'same date alignment expected' — it does NOT enforce it (unlike the sibling alignCloses which aligns by YYYY-MM-DD key). A ticker with a halt-gap or shorter history has its index-N-ago bar on a DIFFERENT calendar date than SPY's, so the ratio %-change (pct1m/3m/6m) and the RS ranking are computed across mismatched dates. LIVE (app/api/sector-rotation + analytics) but mitigated: current callers feed same-calendar US equities (sector ETFs; single ticker vs SPY) so dates line up. Fix = align by date (alignCloses) before the index lookbacks, or intersect the date sets

one_line: KV rate-limiter EXPIRE...NX self-heal swallows its own failure (.catch(()=>{})) and is gated on count===1 so it never re-fires. The narrow conjunction 'the key expired in the SET-NX->INCR gap AND the single heal fetch fails' can strand a TTL-less counter = a PERMANENT per-IP block — the exact failure SET-NX was meant to eliminate. LIVE (all API routes). Fix = check the heal response .ok and fall back to checkRateLimitMemory, or issue EXPIRE unconditionally. Source-verified on origin/main

news-brief id was Buffer.from(link).toString('base64').slice(0,16) — base64 encodes input in independent 3-byte groups so 16 chars = exactly the first 12 bytes of link. Every Yahoo news link shares a 12-byte prefix (https://fina for finance.yahoo.com, https://www. for the rest) so ids collapsed to one value per domain. Client renders briefs with key={brief.id} (app/briefs/page.tsx:75) -> duplicate React keys -> dropped/mis-keyed cards + console error. LIVE on /briefs. FIXED: extracted newsBriefId(link) (lib/api/briefId.ts) encoding the FULL link with base64url (bijection -> zero collisions); +__tests__/api/briefId.test.ts (5 cases incl. proving the old truncation collides). Benchmark-neutral; no schema change (id stays a string)
status: FIXED 2026-06-25 (PR #72 -> main 6ea4293; tsc clean; test 5/5; prod smoke PASS)

one_line: provider OHLC input validation is INCONSISTENT and individually-INCOMPLETE across all three implementors: (1) PolygonProvider.fetchDaily maps r.o/r.h/r.l/r.c with NO finite/positive guard at all; (2) AlphaVantage parseFiniteOrNull guards non-finite but lets a literal 0/negative through (Number.isFinite(0)===true) — its doc-comment misleadingly claims it stops 0-price injection, which it does NOT (it only stops missing->0 coercion); (3) Yahoo skips close==null||close<=0 but a NaN close slips (NaN<=0 is false). A robust provider should skip any bar with a non-finite OR <=0 price. Unify all three to finite-AND-positive (skip the bar). DORMANT (the provider layer has zero prod callers — see A5-DORMANCY) so no live impact; benchmark-neutral. NOT shipping a Polygon-only guard because that would make Polygon stricter than its two siblings = trading one asymmetry for another in dead code
status: MOOT 2026-06-27 — provider layer DELETED (PR #73); the dead code carrying this is gone

one_line: fetchDaily builds each bar from raw open/high/low (fields 1./2./3.) but the ADJUSTED close (field 5. adjusted close), per the AlphaVantage TIME_SERIES_DAILY_ADJUSTED docs where fields 1-4 are as-traded/raw and field 5 is split+dividend adjusted. Across a split or large dividend, the raw OHL are on a different price scale than the adjusted close, so the bar's low can exceed its close (OHLC invariant low<=close<=high violated) and indicators (ATR/returns) see a discontinuity. NOTE: claim rests on AlphaVantage API docs, not on repo types (the response is an untyped Record<string,string> so it is NOT verifiable against a .d.ts like A4-1 was). DORMANT (provider not wired). Fix = scale OHL by factor = adjustedClose/rawClose (field 4. close) so all four are on the adjusted scale, or use raw close (4.) for raw bars
citation: alphavantage.ts:73-76 (1./2./3. raw + 5. adjusted close); per AlphaVantage docs

one_line: _fetchViaCsv (:79) and _fetchViaApi (:99) parse non-missing observation values with parseFloat() and NO finite guard, so a malformed (non-'.' non-numeric) value yields NaN in observations.value (typed number|null). FRED uses '.' for missing (correctly mapped to null), so a NaN requires malformed upstream data = near-zero likelihood, and the live rf consumer (riskFreeRate.ts, Q24) walks backward for the latest non-missing with a val>0 guard so it would skip a NaN anyway. DORMANT-adjacent + benign. Fix = Number.isFinite(parseFloat(val)) ? parsed : null (symmetric to the '.' handling)

CSP-nonce mechanism (Q-040-NEW, self-labelled 'partial') is INCOMPLETE scaffolding and is a LANDMINE on the planned owner ENV-CSP flip. middleware generates a per-request nonce and sets x-nonce on the request, but (a) x-nonce has ZERO consumers repo-wide (grep app/components/lib/hooks — nothing reads it), and (b) when QUANTAN_CSP_ENFORCE=1 the enforced Content-Security-Policy uses script-src 'self' 'nonce-{X}' with NO 'unsafe-inline', set only on the RESPONSE. Next.js App Router always emits inline bootstrap/hydration scripts (which is why next.config's Report-Only policy carries 'unsafe-inline'); with no nonce propagation to those scripts (no x-nonce reader; CSP not on the request headers, which is how Next propagates a nonce per its App-Router CSP pattern), flipping QUANTAN_CSP_ENFORCE=1 would BLOCK Next's own inline scripts -> blank/broken app. The break is overdetermined (enforced nonce-only + zero propagation) regardless of the exact Next mechanism. DORMANT today (flag off -> zero live impact; live CSP is Report-Only, app fine). DISPOSITION (owner, A5-DORMANCY-shaped but lean COMPLETE not delete — the Report-Only->enforce plan shows intent to ship strict CSP): finish the nonce path (consume x-nonce in the root layout to nonce Next's scripts, and/or set the nonce'd CSP on the REQUEST headers) OR remove the dead nonce scaffolding if abandoning strict CSP. VALIDATION: set QUANTAN_CSP_ENFORCE=1 on a PREVIEW deploy + load with console open -> CSP violations on Next inline scripts confirm the break (no prod touch)
status: DEFUSED 2026-07-06 (PR #92) — strict nonce'd CSP now rides the REQUEST headers (Next stamps its inline scripts) and the response serves it Report-Only by default; QUANTAN_CSP_ENFORCE=1 switches to enforcing and is now SAFE after a clean RO window (owner decision; no report-uri collector yet). one_line: the chart data effect re-runs on every `candles` change (every live WebSocket tick) and unconditionally recomputed (calcEMA) + setData ALL ~20 EMA series regardless of visibility. Default visible = 4 (9/20/50/200) so ~16 hidden series did a full per-tick recompute + lightweight-charts series reprocess for lines the user cannot see. FIXED: gated the EMA loop with isEmaLineVisible(indicatorsProp, p) — the same predicate as series creation (L421) and the component visibility sync, mirroring the existing volSma/vwap/bollingerBands gating. Behaviour-preserving: visible EMAs always get data (effect re-runs on tick via `candles` + on toggle via indicatorsProp/visSerialised); hidden skipped; crosshair reads candle OHLCV only; toggle-on re-runs the effect. Frontend, OFF the WR path -> benchmark-neutral
status: FIXED 2026-06-28 (PR #74 -> main 47275f1; tsc clean; KLineChart.test 9/9 incl new KL-6 test; prod smoke PASS)

one_line: useKLineChart's mount effect called an async init() (dynamic import('lightweight-charts') + createChart) fire-and-forget with NO .catch. A chunk-load failure or createChart throw became an UNHANDLED promise rejection — React error boundaries do NOT catch async effect rejections — so the chart silently stayed blank forever, the role=img aria-label was stuck on '(loading)', and chartReadyGen never fired (data effect also never ran). LIVE on every chart (stock/sector/crypto). FIXED: init().catch sets an initError state (+ console.error) surfaced from the hook; the component announces failure in the aria-label ('…failed to load. Try refreshing the page.') and renders a visible role=alert fallback as a SIBLING of the lightweight-charts container (not a child — avoids React reconciling against the imperatively-appended canvas). Behaviour-preserving on the success path
status: FIXED 2026-06-28 (PR #75 -> main 88a8d49; tsc clean; KLineChart.test 10/10 incl new KL-4 test; prod smoke PASS)

one_line: `showRSI` is consumed ONLY in the mount/init effect (deps `[]`, line 622, eslint-disabled) — it sizes the main chart (`:355`) and creates the RSI/MACD/ATR sub-charts (`:464/:502/:533`). So a RUNTIME change to `showRSI` would NOT re-create/destroy those sub-charts or resize the chart (the init effect never re-runs). CONFIRMED LATENT/DORMANT: all three call sites pass a STATIC `showRSI` (JSX shorthand = true) — app/sector/[slug]/page.tsx:502, app/stock/[ticker]/page.tsx:463, components/crypto/BtcChartPanel.tsx:100 — and the main pages also pass `hideTimeframeSelector`, so `showRSI` is never toggled at runtime. No live bug; making it runtime-reactive (move sub-chart creation into a showRSI-keyed effect) is a non-trivial refactor for zero current benefit. Only act if a runtime showRSI toggle is ever added
citation: useKLineChart.ts:622 ([] deps) vs showRSI uses; callers all static
status: open-low (LATENT/dormant — callers static; no runtime toggle path; no fix unless a toggle is added)

one_line: blank-chart boundary coverage gap: on /crypto/btc the default 'chart' tab rendered <BtcChartPanel> UNWRAPPED while the sibling Quant-Lab tab was wrapped in <CryptoChartBoundary>. A render crash in the main BTC chart (BtcChartPanel -> KLineChart) therefore propagated to the ROOT app/error.tsx and blanked the WHOLE /crypto/btc page, instead of degrading to a chart-specific fallback the way the BtcQuantLab tab + the stock/sector pages (ChartErrorBoundary around KLineChart) do. FIXED: wrapped BtcChartPanel in the existing <CryptoChartBoundary title='BTC chart crashed'> (same proven boundary). Behaviour-preserving on the success path (boundary transparent until a child throws); on a crash shows the fallback + retry. F2 also VERIFIED the rest of the chart-boundary coverage is sound (stock/sector wrap KLineChart + each options panel granularly; root app/error.tsx + global-error.tsx backstop)
status: FIXED 2026-06-28 (PR #76 -> main df10657; tsc clean; prod smoke PASS)

one_line: fetch-in-useEffect abort-race: `fetch('/api/sector-rotation').then(setData/setLoading).catch(setError/setLoading)` had NO cancelled-flag / AbortController / cleanup → setState-after-unmount if the panel unmounts before the fetch resolves. React-18-benign (no-op) but the anti-pattern + inconsistent with the 3 already-guarded panels. FIXED: added the cancelled-flag pattern (let cancelled=false; if(cancelled)return before each setState; return ()=>{cancelled=true}) mirroring NewsFeed/LiveBriefClient
status: FIXED 2026-06-30 (PR #77 -> main 8a9d900; tsc clean; prod smoke PASS)

one_line: fetchMetrics/fetchLiq (useCallback) call setState after `await fetchJsonSafe(...)` with no mounted guard. The 30s/60s pollers clearInterval on cleanup (no NEW fetches post-unmount) but an in-flight fetch (initial load or a poll that fired just before unmount) can resolve post-unmount and setState. React-18-benign. FIXED: added a mountedRef (RE-ARMED in the effect body so StrictMode dev double-invoke doesn't leave it false) + gate the post-await setStates and the finally setLoading on mountedRef.current
status: FIXED 2026-06-30 (PR #77 -> main 8a9d900; tsc clean; prod smoke PASS)

the column-sort control was a clickable <span onClick> — NOT focusable and NOT keyboard-operable (no Enter/Space, no role), a WCAG 2.1.1 (Keyboard) + 4.1.2 (Name/Role/Value) violation: keyboard-only users could not sort the backtest results table. Sort STATE was already correct (aria-sort + scope=col on the parent <th>). FIXED: converted the <span> to a real <button type=button> (native focus + Enter/Space), reset button chrome (bg-transparent border-0 p-0) and re-applied uppercase/tracking-wider (Tailwind preflight resets button text-transform)

Found via Vercel runtime-errors.
